message:
test(fcp-streaming): update no_mock_integration constants to MAX_RECONNECT_DELAY=30s (br-y54mi)

Follow-up to ff93c98d which tightened MAX_RECONNECT_DELAY from 60s
to 30s as part of the y54mi reconnect-storm defense. Two assertions
in tests/no_mock_integration.rs still compared against the old 60s
value and started failing on the next test run:

  1. `constants_are_reasonable`: `assert_eq!(MAX_RECONNECT_DELAY,
     Duration::from_secs(60))`
  2. `reconnect_config_default_values`: `assert_eq!(config.max_delay,
     Duration::from_secs(60))` (the default ReconnectConfig pulls
     max_delay from the same constant).

Fix: flip both to `Duration::from_secs(30)` with an inline comment
citing br-y54mi so the next reader finds the bead from the test
source without grepping.

No production code changed.

## Beads

Minor metadata — y54mi stays open pending verification of the
reconnect storm behavior in a live-traffic test on the next staging
cycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-86hnq","title":"[bug] fcp-raptorq: 6 golden erasure recovery tests fail with data mismatch","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CreamReef","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:33:51.946480Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-01T03:06:19.252590Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T21:43:26.308731Z","close_reason":"Stabilized decoder reconstruction order by storing received symbols in ascending ESI order; added reverse-arrival repair regression; light checks passed, rch test blocked by RCH-E326 and local fallback aborted immediately","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2067,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-86hnq","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak deep investigation on 2026-03-27: All 6 golden erasure recovery tests fail with garbled (not rotated) decoded data. The decoded payload has correct length but wrong content — intermediate symbols are solved incorrectly. Root cause is in the Gaussian elimination solver in ConstraintMatrix::solve() (systematic.rs:250-367). The minimum-degree pivot selection with row swapping has a suspected interaction between used_row tracking and physical row swapping, but a targeted fix to the swap sequence didn't resolve the issue. The constraint matrix setup (LDPC/HDPC/LT rows), GF(256) arithmetic, and RFC 6330 tuple expansion are all correct. The encoder works correctly. The InactivationDecoder's peeling + inactivation phases may have a subtle ordering bug when the system is underdetermined (missing source symbols). Next steps: (1) add diagnostic output to compare encoded intermediate symbols vs decoded ones, (2) test with trivial 2-symbol case to isolate minimum reproduction, (3) check if the constraint_symbols() properly extracts equations or if row ordering matters.","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:42:37Z"},{"id":2193,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-86hnq","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak fix on 2026-03-31: The 6 golden erasure recovery test failures are RESOLVED. Root cause was a use-after-move bug in asupersync's SystematicEncoder constructor (/dp/asupersync/src/raptorq/systematic.rs:888). The `params` struct was moved into `Self` at line 882 and then `params.k_prime` was read at line 888 to set `next_repair_esi`. This caused `next_repair_esi` to be garbage, which meant repair symbols were generated with wrong ESIs, making erasure recovery impossible. Fix: read `k_prime` before moving `params`. Full raptorq suite: 739/739 pass.","created_at":"2026-04-01T03:06:19Z"}]}

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-8a2wv","title":"fcp-host capability verification trusts rollbackable wall clock for expiry","description":"Capability verification in fcp-host derives temporal validity directly from `Utc::now()` and then treats that wall-clock result as authoritative for live auth.

Evidence:
- `crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs:3891-3912` computes `currently_valid` from `Utc::now()` with no monotonic floor or rollback guard.
- `crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs:4041-4050` re-checks not-before against another fresh `Utc::now()`.
- `crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:1509-1530` accepts `persisted_verify.valid` as a live execution gate.

Concrete failure:
1. Issue a capability token with a 60-second TTL.
2. Let it expire in real time.
3. Move the host clock backwards by 2 minutes.
4. `verify_capability_token` recomputes `currently_valid = true`, and `verify_live_request` admits an already-expired token.

This is a time-travel auth bypass. Expiry needs a monotonic last-seen floor, persisted max-observed time, or explicit rollback/skew rejection.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:00:00Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:00:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp-host","security","auth","time"]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cdj","title":"fcp.1password: Credential Management Connector","description":"## Overview
Secure credential management connector for 1Password integration, enabling AI agents to safely access and manage secrets without exposing sensitive data.

## Core Capabilities

### Item Lookup (Read-Only by Default)
- Search vault items by name, tags, or categories
- Retrieve specific fields (username, URL, notes) without exposing passwords
- Read-only access as the default permission level
- Explicit elevation required for password retrieval

### Password Generation
- Generate strong passwords with configurable requirements
- Support for length, character sets, and complexity rules
- Memorable password generation (word-based)
- PIN generation for numeric-only contexts

### Entry Creation
- Create new login entries with generated passwords
- Support for secure notes, credit cards, identities
- Custom field support for application-specific data
- Automatic categorization and tagging

### Vault Organization
- List and navigate vault structure
- Move items between vaults
- Bulk tagging operations
- Archive and restore items

### Secure Note Access
- Read secure notes for configuration data
- Structured note parsing (YAML, JSON embedded)
- Document attachment retrieval
- Version history access

## Security Requirements

### Credential Protection
- **CRITICAL**: Never expose credentials in logs or output
- Mask all sensitive fields in debug output
- Use secure memory handling
- Clear credentials from memory after use

### Time-Limited Credential Leases
- Short-lived access tokens (5-minute default)
- Automatic credential rotation
- Lease renewal with re-authentication
- Audit trail for all credential access

## Implementation Notes
- Use 1Password CLI (op) or Connect API
- Support both personal and team accounts
- Service account integration for automation
- MFA handling for interactive sessions

## Testing Requirements
- Mock vault for unit tests
- Integration tests with test vault
- Security audit for credential handling
- Penetration testing for leak prevention","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:48.804778945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T07:34:56.145167808Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T07:34:56.145167808Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-8egw","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Evidence:
- `crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs:650-665` exposes `not_before_delay_secs` as unbounded `u64`.
- `crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs:3719-3721` computes `not_before` with `i64::try_from(delay).unwrap_or(0)`.

Concrete failure:
1. Send an issuance request with `not_before_delay_secs = 18446744073709551615`.
2. The `u64 -> i64` conversion fails.
3. `unwrap_or(0)` collapses the delay to zero.
4. The minted token becomes valid immediately instead of being deferred or rejected.

This is a fail-open time gate in capability issuance; overflow should reject the request or clamp safely, not mint an immediately-usable token.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:00:00Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:00:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp-host","security","auth","time"]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cmkuk","title":"fcp-discord: invalid embeds input is silently discarded in send/edit paths","description":"## Finding
Both `invoke_send_message()` and `invoke_edit_message()` parse `input["embeds"]` with `serde_json::from_value(...).ok()` ([connectors/discord/src/connector.rs:1338-1346](connectors/discord/src/connector.rs:1338), [1448-1455](connectors/discord/src/connector.rs:1448)).

Because the deserialization error is discarded, malformed embed payloads are treated as `None` instead of producing a typed request error. In `send_message`, that falls through to the generic `Either `content` or `embeds` must be provided` branch; in `edit_message`, malformed embeds bypass embed validation entirely and the edit proceeds as though no embeds were supplied.

## Why this matters
Agents get misleading validation feedback and can accidentally send/edit messages after supplying malformed embeds. This is a silent input-shape downgrade, not strict request validation.

## Recommendation

    fn maybe_reset_window(&mut self) {
        let elapsed = self.window_start.elapsed();
        if elapsed >= self.config.config.window {
            self.count = 0;
            self.window_start = Instant::now();
        }

Classic fixed-window exploit: an attacker consumes `limit` requests at T = window - epsilon, then consumes another `limit` at T = window + epsilon. Net: 2 * limit within 2 * epsilon seconds, concentrated at the boundary. The connector appears to have honored "limit per window" but sustained ~2x throughput during the boundary instant.

TokenBucket in fcp-ratelimit uses a continuous refill model and does not have this property. PoolState is the tracker exposed to connector SDK users via RateLimitTracker::from_declarations, so this is the path most manifest-declared rate limits actually hit.

Fix options:
  (a) Port PoolState to sliding-window log or leaky-bucket (accurate, more memory)
  (b) Port PoolState to rolling-window approximation (count_prev * fraction + count_curr)
  (c) Delegate PoolState to TokenBucket under the hood

Option (b) is cheapest and typically sufficient: two counters (prev, curr) with a linear interpolation.

### Snapshot Redaction Rules
```rust
/// Fields to redact for stable snapshots
const REDACT_FIELDS: &[&str] = &[
    "timestamp",
    "created_at",
    "updated_at",
    "trace_id",
    "correlation_id",
    "duration_ms",
    "node_id",      // May vary in tests
    "session_id",
];

fn redact_dynamic_fields(json: &mut Value) {
    for field in REDACT_FIELDS {
        if let Some(v) = json.get_mut(field) {
            *v = Value::String("[REDACTED]".to_string());
        }
    }
}
```

### Terminal Output Snapshots
```rust
#[test]
fn test_doctor_healthy_output() {
    let output = Command::new("fcp")
        .args(["doctor", "--no-color"])
        .output()
        .expect("failed to run");
    
    // Strip ANSI for deterministic snapshots
    let clean = strip_ansi_escapes::strip(&output.stdout)?;
    let text = String::from_utf8_lossy(&clean);
    
    // Redact timestamps and IDs
    let redacted = redact_terminal_output(&text);
    insta::assert_snapshot!(redacted);
}
```

```rust
    /// Environment overrides
    env: HashMap<String, String>,
    /// Captured output
    output_log: Vec<OutputEntry>,
}

impl CliTestHarness {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        let temp_home = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
        let mock_server = MockServer::start().await;
        
        let mut env = HashMap::new();
        env.insert("FCP_HOME".into(), temp_home.path().display().to_string());
        env.insert("FCP_MESH_URL".into(), mock_server.uri());
        env.insert("NO_COLOR".into(), "1".into());
        
        Self { temp_home, mock_server, env, output_log: vec![] }
    }
    
    pub fn run(&mut self, args: &[&str]) -> CommandOutput {
        let output = Command::new(fcp_binary())
            .args(args)
            .envs(&self.env)
            .output()
            .expect("failed to run fcp");
        
        self.output_log.push(OutputEntry {
            args: args.iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect(),
            exit_code: output.status.code(),
            stdout: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string(),
            stderr: String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string(),
        });
        
        CommandOutput::from(output)
    }
}
```

## CI Integration

### Full CI Workflow
```yaml
cli-tests:
  strategy:
    matrix:
      os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-14, windows-latest]
  runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Install Rust
      uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
    
    - name: Cache cargo
      uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
    
    - name: Security audit
      run: cargo audit --deny warnings
    
    - name: Run unit tests
      run: cargo test --package fcp-cli --lib
      env:
        RUST_LOG: debug
        RUST_BACKTRACE: 1
    
    - name: Run integration tests
      run: cargo test --package fcp-cli --test integration
      env:
        E2E_SEED: 42
    
    - name: Check snapshots
      run: cargo insta test --check
    
    - name: Coverage report
      run: cargo llvm-cov --package fcp-cli --json --output-path coverage.json
    
    - name: Verify coverage threshold
      run: |
        COVERAGE=$(jq ".data[0].totals.lines.percent" coverage.json)
        if (( $(echo "$COVERAGE < 80" | bc -l) )); then
          echo "Coverage $COVERAGE% is below 80% threshold"
          exit 1
        fi
```

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-e9nh.1","title":"TEST: fcp new (scaffold/check) command","description":"# TEST: `fcp new` (scaffold/check) command

## Goal
Add dedicated tests for the `fcp new` developer command to ensure scaffolding is safe, deterministic, and produces compliant defaults.

## Why a separate test bead?
`fcp new` is a high-leverage DX feature for building many connectors, but it is not required for the operator MVP commands.
Keeping its tests separate prevents it from blocking MVP CLI validation while still enforcing correctness when we implement it.

## Test matrix

### 1) Scaffold creation
- Creates expected directory structure under `connectors/<name>/`.
- Emits a valid connector manifest with:
  - single-zone binding
  - default-deny NetworkConstraints
  - no secret material present
- Generates placeholder operations + schemas.

### 2) Safety / no-overwrite behavior
- Fails (or requires explicit `--force` if we add one) when target directory exists.
- Never deletes files (NO rm/unlink behaviors).
- `--dry-run` prints intended outputs without touching the filesystem.

### 3) `--check` validation
- Detects malformed manifest.
- Detects missing required template sections.
- Detects obvious secret anti-patterns (plaintext token examples, etc.).
- Emits actionable diagnostics with stable error codes.

### 4) Output + logging
- Supports `--json` with a stable output schema.
- Logs include correlation ids and do not leak secrets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests run deterministically in CI.
- Passing tests prove:
  - safe scaffold behavior
  - manifest correctness defaults
  - no overwrites/deletions
  - `--json` schema stability

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fqzmp","title":"fcp-mesh/node: planner zones never populated (production zone-policy filtering broken)","description":"**File**: crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:792, 801

**Code**:
```rust
fn build_planner_input(&self, now_ms: u64) -> PlannerInput {
    if let Some(profile) = &self.local_profile {
        nodes.push(NodeInfo {
            ...
            zones: Vec::new(), // TODO: populate from mesh enrollment data
        });
    }
    for state in self.peers.values() {
        nodes.push(NodeInfo {
            ...
            zones: Vec::new(), // TODO: populate from peer zone advertisements
        });
    }
}
```

**Root cause**: `MeshNode::build_planner_input` hardcodes `zones: Vec::new()` for both the local node and every peer. The TODOs mark this as never being wired to real enrollment data or peer advertisements.

**Impact**: `PlannerInput → NodeInfo.zones` is threaded into `CandidateNode.zones` (planner.rs:537). Planner zone-policy filtering (planner.rs:1852 `zone_policy_rejects_nodes_with_unknown_zones`, 1806 `zone_policy_filters_candidates_by_zone`) keys off this field. In production, every candidate has empty zones, so:
- `zone_policy` set to allow only `z:work` rejects ALL candidates (no node has any zone)
- `zone_policy` set to deny `z:public` allows ALL candidates (no node is in z:public from the policy's view)
- The planner cannot enforce zone-affinity placement at all

This is silently broken: no error, no warning, just universally-bypassed filtering.

**Fix plan**:
1. Plumb local zones from the existing MeshIdentity / MeshConfig source-of-truth (likely `identity.tags` filtered through ZoneTagMapping::tag_to_zone) into local_profile or a sibling local_zones field on MeshNode.
2. Track per-peer zones from the gossip / handshake path. Peers already advertise tags via their MeshIdentity in handshake; add zone derivation alongside symbol-summary handling.
3. Replace both `zones: Vec::new()` sites with the real source.
4. Add an integration test in fcp-mesh: build_planner_input populates non-empty zones for local node when MeshIdentity has fcp tags; peer state with attested fcp tags appears in planner candidates with matching zones.
5. Add a planner-level test that demonstrates zone-policy filtering actually filters once zones flow through correctly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:39:48.973287Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:21:17.298362Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:21:17.297599Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit a75b9e9d: fcp-mesh/node thread local and peer zones through build_planner_input","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2798,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fqzmp","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fix landed in a75b9e9d by another swarm agent — independently arrived at the same design (local_zones HashSet on MeshNode, update_local_zones setter, both build_planner_input sites wired, same regression test shape).  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","title":"fcp.airtable: Attachment upload and download handling","description":"# fcp.airtable: Attachment Upload and Download Handling

## Goal
Handle Airtable attachments safely under strict sandboxing.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.attachments.upload`
- `airtable.attachments.get` (fetch attachment metadata/URL)
- optional: `airtable.attachments.download` (if we choose to implement actual download)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated:
  - read vs write separated
- NetworkConstraints:
  - Airtable API host(s) allowed
  - attachment download hosts must be explicitly enumerated/pinned
  - validate URLs are HTTPS and match allowlist
- Data handling:
  - attachments move as **ObjectId references** (no local disk persistence)
  - enforce size limits and streaming bounds
- Logging:
  - never log attachment contents
  - URLs may contain opaque tokens; treat as sensitive

## Tests
- Allow/deny matrix for attachment URL hosts.
- Size limit and timeout tests.
- Redaction tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Attachment flows are safe-by-default and deterministic.
- Non-allowed hosts are blocked by Network Guard.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fytk7","title":"fcp-tailscale exports MockTailscaleClient from production lib surface","description":"`crates/fcp-tailscale/src/lib.rs` publicly reexports `MockTailscaleClient`, and `crates/fcp-tailscale/src/client.rs` declares it as a normal `pub struct` with public constructors/helpers.
